steps.title: Identify the coefficient
steps.content: In $H=2.403L+23.31$, $H$ is estimated height and $L$ is femur length. The number $2.403$ is the coefficient of $L$.

steps.title: Interpret the slope
steps.content: For a linear model $H=mL+b$, the coefficient $m$ tells how much $H$ changes when $L$ increases by $1$. Here, $m=2.403$, so the estimated height increases by $2.403$ inches for each additional inch of femur length.

steps.title: Eliminate reversed interpretations
steps.content: Choice B reverses the variables: it describes change in femur length per inch of height. Choice C also reverses the variables and incorrectly uses the intercept $23.31$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D describes the whole expression $H$, not the coefficient $2.403$. Choice A correctly says $2.403$ is the increase in estimated height for each 1-inch increase in femur length, so A is correct.
